title: bootstrap korczewski-ha cluster + re-seal secrets
description:
## Summary
- Bootstrapped platform prereqs on the new standalone **korczewski-ha** cluster (3-node k3s 1.35.4 HA on pk-hetzner-4/6/8): sealed-secrets controller (helm, ns `sealed-secrets`), cert-manager (helm, ns `cert-manager` with `podDnsPolicy=Default`), cert-manager-lego-webhook (DNS-01 via ipv64), ClusterIssuer `letsencrypt-prod`, and `ipv64-api-key` Secret in `cert-manager` ns.
- Re-sealed `environments/sealed-secrets/korczewski.yaml` against the new cluster's sealing certificate. Updated `environments/certs/korczewski.pem` to the new pubkey.
- `environments/korczewski.yaml` deliberately **not modified** — `context:` still resolves to `mentolder` so day-to-day tooling keeps routing to the unified cluster until the cutover task lands.

This is task 4 + 5 of the korczewski cluster-split migration. Task 6 (DNS) and 7 (`workspace:deploy`) follow in separate PRs.

## Verification
- `kubectl --context korczewski-ha get pods -n sealed-secrets` → `sealed-secrets-…` Running.
- `kubectl --context korczewski-ha get pods -n cert-manager` → controller + cainjector + webhook + lego-webhook Running.
- `kubectl --context korczewski-ha get clusterissuer letsencrypt-prod` → `True`, "ACME account registered".
- `kubectl --context korczewski-ha apply --dry-run=server -f environments/sealed-secrets/korczewski.yaml` → `ipv64-api-key` server-dry-run created (proves decryption works); other entries report NotFound on `workspace-korczewski`/`coturn`/`website-korczewski`/`workspace-office` namespaces, which are created during a later `workspace:deploy`.

## Test plan
- [ ] Confirm CI passes (`task test:all`).
- [ ] Defer real ACME issuance + workspace deploy to task 7.
